cb_step_1.title: Select the Pelvic CT Scan
cb_step_1.simple: Choose the pelvic CT volume from the available options.
cb_step_1.detailed: This step identifies the pelvic CT scan that will be used for segmentation and planning. Correct selection ensures the fracture is analyzed on the proper imaging study. In the dropdown menu, select the volume labeled as your pelvic CT.

cb_step_2.title: Segment the Pelvis
cb_step_2.simple: Click the 'Run Step 1: Segment Pelvis' button to automatically segment the pelvic bone.
cb_step_2.detailed: This step automatically creates a 3D model of the intact pelvic bone from your CT scan. The pelvic segmentation provides the anatomical reference for aligning fractured fragments later. Press the button and wait for the segmentation to complete.

cb_step_3.title: Detect Fracture Fragments
cb_step_3.simple: Click the 'Run Step 2: Segment Fractures' button to identify the individual fracture fragments.
cb_step_3.detailed: This step separates the broken pelvic fragments from the main bone, creating individual segmentations for each piece. Accurate fragment detection is essential for planning reduction and screw placement. Press the button to automatically detect and label all visible fracture fragments.

cb_step_4.title: Select Fragments for Planning
cb_step_4.simple: Untick any fracture fragments you wish to exclude from the reduction plan.
cb_step_4.detailed: Some small or irrelevant fragments may not need to be included in the reduction plan. Unchecking a fragment removes it from the template generation and reduction steps, focusing planning on the clinically important pieces. Review the list and uncheck fragments that should be ignored, then confirm.

cb_step_5.title: Generate Reduction Template
cb_step_5.simple: Click the 'Run Step 3: Generate Template' button to create a reduction template based on the selected fragments.
cb_step_5.detailed: This step uses the intact pelvis and the selected fracture fragments to compute a template for reducing the fracture back to anatomical alignment. The template serves as the target for the subsequent registration and reduction step. Press the button to compute the template.

cb_step_6.title: Register and Reduce Fracture
cb_step_6.simple: Click the 'Run Step 4: Register Reduce' button to automatically align the fracture fragments to the template.
cb_step_6.detailed: This step performs automatic registration of each fragment to the reduction template, effectively reducing the fracture. The result is a 3D model showing the reassembled pelvis with fragments in their correct anatomical positions. Press the button to initiate the reduction.

cb_step_7.title: Decide on Manual Adjustment
cb_step_7.simple: Choose whether you need to fine-tune any fragment position manually.
cb_step_7.detailed: After automatic reduction, some fragments may still be slightly misaligned. Manual adjustment allows you to fine-tune the position and rotation of individual fragments using interactive 3D tools. If the reduction looks satisfactory, you can skip straight to screw planning. Check the box 'Manually adjust a fragment' if you need to make corrections, otherwise leave it unchecked and proceed.

cb_step_8.title: Select Fragment to Adjust
cb_step_8.simple: From the fragment list, choose the one that needs manual adjustment.
cb_step_8.detailed: Each fracture fragment is listed individually. Selecting a fragment identifies it as the target for position and rotation adjustments. Pick the fragment whose alignment you want to refine.

cb_step_9.title: Reposition the Fragment
cb_step_9.simple: Use the 3D view and transformation tools to move and rotate the selected fragment into correct alignment.
cb_step_9.detailed: This step allows you to manually drag, rotate, or translate the selected fragment to its proper anatomical position. Use the interactive handles or transformation widgets in the 3D viewer. Once the fragment looks correctly aligned relative to the intact pelvis, confirm your changes.

cb_step_10.title: Apply Manual Adjustments
cb_step_10.simple: Click the 'Apply adjustments' button to save your manual repositioning changes.
cb_step_10.detailed: After you have moved the fragment, applying the adjustments integrates the new position into the reduction plan. This ensures your manual corrections are included in the final model for screw planning. Press the button to commit the changes.

cb_step_11.title: Plan Screw Trajectories
cb_step_11.simple: Click the 'Run Step 5: Plan Screws' button to automatically generate candidate screw trajectories.
cb_step_11.detailed: This step analyzes the reduced pelvis model and computes optimal screw paths for fixation. The planned screws represent the surgical hardware that will stabilize the fracture. Press the button to generate screw trajectories.

cb_step_12.title: Decide on Screw Editing
cb_step_12.simple: Choose whether you need to manually adjust any screw trajectories.
cb_step_12.detailed: The automatically generated screw paths may need fine-tuning for safety or anatomical fit. Editing allows you to modify the position and orientation of individual screws. If the trajectories look perfect, you can finish the planning here. Check the box 'Edit Screw trajectories' to make adjustments, or leave it unchecked to conclude.

cb_step_13.title: Fine-Tune Screw Trajectories
cb_step_13.simple: Use the 3D view to adjust the position and angle of each screw trajectory as needed.
cb_step_13.detailed: Each screw trajectory appears as a line in the 3D view. You can click and drag to reposition its entry point or change its angle to avoid critical structures like nerves or vessels. Ensure the screw path stays within the bone corridor. Confirm after each adjustment.

cb_step_14.title: Regenerate Screws from Edits
cb_step_14.simple: Click the 'Regenerate screws from edited lines' button to update the screw models based on your manual adjustments.
cb_step_14.detailed: After editing the trajectories, this step recalculates the final screw geometry (length, diameter, etc.) to match your adjusted paths. It also updates any associated measurements or visualizations. Press the button to rebuild the screws from your edits.

cb_step_15.title: Finalize Screw Planning
cb_step_15.simple: Uncheck the 'Edit Screw trajectories' checkbox to lock in your final screw plan.
cb_step_15.detailed: This step confirms that you are finished editing screw trajectories. Unchecking the box closes the editing mode and finalizes the planning output. After this, the plan is complete and ready for export or surgical use.
